## Summary

Three fixes to reduce signal-loss-to-reconnection time and prevent the progress bar from going backward when a rescue/rekick occurs:

1. **Null-pulse orphan threshold: 60s → 30s** — Workers that crash before their first pulse are now detected in half the time. Worst-case reconnection drops from ~125s to ~95s.

2. **Code fallback max_attempts aligned to DB default (11)** — The `?? 3` fallbacks in `processor.ts` and `jobStore.supabase.ts` now match the DB column default of 11 (initial claim + up to 10 watchdog rescues). This was a code/schema drift — the DB migration `20260521095500` raised it to 11 but the TypeScript fallbacks weren't updated.

3. **Progress bar regression fix** — `progress_high_water` from the progress JSONB is now seeded into the initial poller job on page load. Previously, the display-side monotonic ratchet existed but had no high-water value on first render, so a page refresh after a rescue could show the bar at a lower position.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `app/api/workers/watchdog/route.ts` — orphan threshold constant + comments
- `lib/evaluation/processor.ts` — max_attempts fallback alignment
- `lib/jobs/jobStore.supabase.ts` — max_attempts fallback alignment
- `app/evaluate/[jobId]/page.tsx` — seed `progress_high_water` into initial poller job

**Does NOT touch:**
- Evaluation pipeline logic, prompts, or scoring
- Database schema or migrations
- The display-layer monotonic ratchet (already correct in `evaluation-poller-display.ts`)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Phase 0 retains its 90s grace period (calibration legitimately takes 12-30s) — only the null-pulse orphan threshold was tightened.
- The 30s threshold is still conservative: a healthy worker claims → loads manuscript → starts processing in under 15s. If a worker is genuinely slow to start (network latency, cold start), it will pulse within 30s.
- Cron interval is still 60s (Vercel minimum), so worst-case detection is still 60s + 30s = 90s. Further tightening requires a different trigger mechanism.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no evaluation prompts, scoring, or phase logic changed
